# Project Instructions

## Overview
macOS menu bar utility app (SwiftUI + AppKit) with 16 independent feature modules. Swift 5.0, macOS 15.5+ deployment target. Single external dependency: WhisperKit.

## Code Style

### Naming
- Files: `{FeatureName}Model.swift`, `{FeatureName}View.swift`, `{FeatureName}OverlayView.swift`
- Models: `@MainActor final class {FeatureName}Model: ObservableObject`
- Views: `struct {FeatureName}View: View` with `let onBack: () -> Void`
- Enums: `PascalCase` type, `camelCase` cases, prefer `String, CaseIterable` conformance
- Booleans: `is`-prefixed (`isEnabled`, `isRunning`)

### Patterns
- Use `@Published` for observable state; `@Published private(set)` for read-only derived state
- Use `private` for internal helpers, timers, and buffers
- No explicit `public`/`internal` — rely on Swift defaults
- Use `// MARK: - Section` in model files to organize sections (Settings, Runtime State, Monitoring, etc.)
- No doc comments — rely on self-documenting names

### Persistence
- Use `UserDefaults.standard` with dot-notation keys: `"{featureName}.{property}"`
- Persist in `didSet` on `@Published` properties; load in `init()`
- Use `JSONEncoder`/`JSONDecoder` for complex types — no `@AppStorage`, no Core Data

### Error Handling
- Graceful degradation: log errors, don't propagate to UI
- Use `NSLog("[ModuleName] ...")` for error logging (preferred over `print`)
- Use `try?` for non-critical file operations
- Redirect process stderr to `FileHandle.nullDevice`

### Async
- Annotate all model classes `@MainActor`
- Use `Timer.scheduledTimer` with `[weak self]` + `Task { @MainActor in }` inside callbacks
- Use `withCheckedContinuation` to bridge `Foundation.Process` to async
- No Combine — use `@Published` properties directly

## Architecture
- **MVVM**: Model (`ObservableObject`) + View (SwiftUI struct), no service layer
- **Feature isolation**: Each feature in `MacPowerToys/Features/{Name}/` — no cross-feature imports
- **State injection**: App creates all `@StateObject` models, passes via `.environmentObject()`
- **Navigation**: `Feature` enum in `ContentView` drives state-based navigation with `.easeInOut(duration: 0.15)`
- **Overlay windows**: NSView subclasses in `*OverlayView.swift` for screen-level features
- See `.blink/architecture.md` for full module map and data flow

## UI Design System
- Font design: `.rounded` everywhere
- Button style: `.buttonStyle(.plain)`, toggle: `.toggleStyle(.switch)`, control size: `.mini`
- Spacing: `12` primary VStack, `6-10` inner groups
- Navigation header: Back button (`chevron.left`) + title (`.headline, .rounded`) + Spacer + Divider
- Card backgrounds: `.white.opacity(0.14)`, strokes: `.white.opacity(0.18)`
- All views accept `let onBack: () -> Void` for navigation

## Testing
- XCTest, files in `MacPowerToysTests/`, naming: `{ClassName}Tests.swift`
- Test methods: `test{Behavior}()`, use `@MainActor` when testing `@MainActor` models
- Coverage is minimal — expand when adding new features
- See `.blink/testing.md` for full details

## Dependencies
- Only external package: **WhisperKit** (SPM, ≥ 0.15.0) for speech-to-text
- **FFmpeg**: optional runtime dependency via Homebrew — invoked through `Foundation.Process`
- No SwiftLint, SwiftFormat, or CI/CD configured

## Build & Release
- Build: `xcodebuild -project MacPowerToys.xcodeproj -scheme MacPowerToys`
- Release: `./release.sh <version>` — builds, signs, creates DMG
- Bundle ID: `com.rbinar.MacPowerToys`
